title: Introduction and Research Context
objective: Establish the research domain of pedestrian crossing behavior at signalized intersections, framing the importance of understanding both traditional crossing violations and emerging autonomous vehicle interactions

title: Methodological Approaches in Pedestrian Crossing Research
objective: Survey and categorize the methodological families employed in pedestrian crossing behavior research, including VR experiments, observational studies, and statistical modeling techniques

title: External Interface Design for Autonomous Vehicles
objective: Synthesize findings from VR-based studies on pedestrian interactions with fully autonomous vehicles, evaluating external feature preferences and their effects on crossing decisions

title: Factors Influencing Pedestrian Red-Light Running Behavior
objective: Compare and contrast statistical modeling approaches and findings from red-light running studies across different geographic contexts, examining personal, environmental, and social factors

title: Cross-Study Comparison and Claim Synthesis
objective: Identify and analyze unresolved claim differences across related tasks, examining contradictions in gender effects, age effects, and environmental factor impacts between studies

title: Research Gaps and Future Directions
objective: Synthesize identified methodological and empirical gaps, proposing clearer comparison criteria and highlighting opportunities for integrated research on pedestrian crossing behavior
